title.en: You bet! Of course! 그럼요! 당연하죠!

description.en: When you readily accept a request or express strong affirmation! Learn the natural uses of "Geureom-yo" and "Dang-yeon-ha-jo".
description.ko: 상대방의 부탁을 흔쾌히 들어주거나 강한 긍정을 표현할 때! "그럼요"와 "당연하죠"의 자연스러운 쓰임새를 알아봅니다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world. Welcome back to Talkori. I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕하세요 여러분~! 오늘도 여러분과 함께해서 행복한 미나입니다.
content.translation: Hello everyone~! I'm Mina. happy to be with you again today.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. I'm going to a party tonight. and I want to look my best. Could you help me pick a tie.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오 그럼요~! 제가 옷 고르는 건 전문이라구요!
content.translation: Oh~ 'Of course~!' I'm an expert at picking out clothes!

insight.title: 그럼요
insight.usage_tip: A polite way to show agreement or willingness to help.

content.speaker: Mina
content.translation: Naturally~! John. you're my best friend!

insight.title: 당연하죠
insight.usage_tip: Used when something is obvious or a matter of course.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "You bet!" I knew I could count on you.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "그럼요" and "당연하죠". They both sound so positive.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! "그럼요"는 상대방의 말에 기분 좋게 동의할 때 정말 많이 써요.
content.translation: Exactly! 'Geureom-yo' is used a lot when you happily agree with someone.

content.speaker: John
content.translation: You bet...,

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 조금 더 확실하게 긍정하고 싶을 땐. "당연하죠" 라고 해보세요.
content.translation: If you want to be even more certain. try 'Dang-yeon-ha-jo'.

content.speaker: John
content.translation: Of course~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Is there a formal way to say "Of course" in a business setting.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐. "물론이죠" 라고 하면 아주 완벽합니다!
content.translation: In that case. saying 'Mul-ron-i-jo' is perfect!

content.speaker: John
content.translation: Certainly...,

content.speaker: John
content.text: Imagine we are at a Korean restaurant. and the side dishes are so delicious. I want to ask for more Kimchi.

content.speaker: Mina
content.translation: Student~ shall I give you more Kimchi~?

content.speaker: John
content.translation: You bet~! The Kimchi here is really delicious!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아이고 잘 먹네~! 우리 식당 단골 될 거죠?
content.translation: Oh. you eat so well~! You'll be a regular at our restaurant. won't you?

content.speaker: John
content.translation: Of course~! I'll come tomorrow too!

content.speaker: Mina
content.translation: Nice~! Then this is on the house!

content.speaker: John
content.translation: Wow~ 'Certainly!' Thank you. ma'am!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존 씨는 김치만 있으면 어디든 갈 수 있을 것 같아요.
content.translation: It seems like John can go anywhere as long as there is Kimchi.

content.speaker: John
content.text: These reactions make me feel so much more confident.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 누군가의 부탁에..., "그럼요!"..., 라고 답하면 대화가 훨씬 부드러워져요!
content.translation: Right. Answering 'Geureom-yo!' to someone's request makes the conversation much smoother!

content.speaker: John
content.translation: You bet!

content.speaker: John
content.translation: Of course!

content.speaker: John
content.translation: Certainly!

content.speaker: John
content.translation: Gladly!

insight.title: 기꺼이요
insight.usage_tip: A very polite way to show you are happy to do a favor.

content.speaker: John
content.translation: Of course! / It goes without saying!

insight.title: 두말하면 잔소리죠
insight.description: An idiom meaning 'No need to ask twice'
insight.usage_tip: Used when something is so obvious that talking about it further is just nagging.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 누군가 도움을 청하면 시원하게 외쳐보세요.
content.translation: Everyone. if someone asks for help. shout out coolly. 'Geureom-yo!'

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. We'll be back with another helpful episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 그럼요. 제가 도와드릴게요. (Of course. I'll help you.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be natural. To be obvious
vocabulary.example: 그건 당연한 결과예요. (That is a natural result.)
기꺼이 수락하겠습니다. (I will gladly accept it.)
저는 이 카페 단골이에요. (I'm a regular at this cafe.)

vocabulary.meaning: On the house. Free of charge (Konglish)
vocabulary.example: 이건 서비스로 드리는 거예요. (I'm giving this to you for free.)
